Leonard P. Oliveira, 65, died unexpectedly Sunday, December 23rd, at Charlton Memorial Hospital, surrounded by all his family and loved ones.  He is survived by his wife of 44 years, Virginia (Xavier) Oliveira of New Bedford. Born in New Bedford, son of the late Joaquim P. Oliveira and Alice (Costa) Oliveira,  he lived in New Bedford all his life.  He was presently employed by C&S Wholesale Grocers.  Mr. Oliveira worked in the grocery business for over forty years.  His retail career included People’s, Fernandes, Superplace, and SuperValu.  He was a communicant of St. Mary’s Church, New Bedford, and served as a Eucharistic minister for many years.  His love for his family and friends was his greatest gift.  His generosity and patience were unlimited and he impacted the lives of everyone he touched.  Mr. Oliveira enjoyed fishing, horseshoes, and entertaining everyone around him with his endless humor and quick wit. Survivors include his wife; eight children: Susan, Robert and his wife, Tracy, Donna Gadbois, and her husband Paul, all of Dartmouth; Deborah, and her husband, James Shuey, of New Orleans; David, and his wife, Susan, of New Bedford; Paul, and his wife, Christine, of Westport; Michael, and his wife, Sarah, of Uxbridge; and Kenneth, and his wife, Tricia, of South Boston; 12 grandchildren: Karissa, Jocelyn, Thomas, Zachary, Jacob, Cory, Sydney, Lindsey, Ana, Jack, Kate, and Rachel; two brothers: Jack Oliveira of Fairhaven, and Sil Oliveira of  St. Petersburg, FL; and a sister, Alice Botelho of Acushnet.  He is also survived by many nieces, nephews, and cousins.  He was the uncle of the late Jimmy Xavier and Matty Oliveira. Funeral from the Saunders-Dwyer Mattapoisett Home for Funerals, 50 County Rd. (Rt. 6) Mattapoisett Thursday, December 27th at 8:45 AM. Funeral Mass in St. Mary's Church, Tarkiln Hill Rd at 10 AM.  Burial will follow in St. Mary's Cemetery.  Visiting hours will be on Wednesday, December 26th from 3-8 PM.  In accordance with his wishes, in lieu of flowers, donations may be made to the Matty Oliveira Memorial Fund, 17 Holiday Drive, Fairhaven, MA  02719.

(Ganley) Wrenn.  Mrs. Adie graduated from Lowell High School in the class of 1943.  In her free time she loved reading and was a self professed news junkie who read two newspapers every day.  She enjoyed working in the yard, gardening, and was a wonderful cook.  Mrs. Adie also took pleasure in traveling with her husband, especially to Ogunquit, ME.   Mrs. Adie was a devout catholic who loved her home, her family, and the town of Dracut. She is survived by her four children and their spouses,  Geralyn and Ronald Gelina of Charlotte,  NC,  Robert and Kathleen Adie of Henderson, NV, Joanne and Henry Altenweg of Poestenkill, NY , James and Susan Adie of Hudson, NH; five grandsons, Matthew Gelina of SC and Daniel Gelina of NC, Michael, Christopher and Antonio Adie of Hudson, NH; her sister, Sheila McCartney of Lawrence; her sister-in-laws, Mary Ellen Adie of Dracut and Anne Adie of Dracut; twenty-one nephews and nieces. She was also the sister of the late Gerald Wrenn and the late Anna Linehan. Friends may call at the DRACUT FUNERAL HOME, 2159 Lakeview Avenue, DRACUT, on Friday from 4:00 until 8:00 PM.  Her funeral will be held from the funeral home on Saturday at 8:00 AM followed by her Funeral Mass to be celebrated at 9:00 AM at Ste. Therese Church in Dracut.  Interment will be in St. Mary Cemetery in Tewksbury.  Flowers may be sent or memorial contributions may be made in her name to the St. Jude Children's Research Hospital, 332 N. Lauderdale, Memphis, TN 38105.

Towle, Richard R. of Quincy, formerly of Norwell and Hingham died December 23, 2007. Devoted husband of the late Dorothy L. (Kannaly) Towle.  Beloved father of Richard R. James Francis Baptista will be remembered most for his quick wit, humor, always plotting his next joke, and his love of a good story to tell for he had many. He was a great and loving friend to all, an extraordinary person who left a lasting impression on all he met. He died on December 22, 2007 at MetroWest Medical Center, Framingham Union Hospital at the age of 88. The cause was colon cancer.  He led a remarkable and valiant battle against the disease for almost 4 years. He held the job of assistant deputy harbor master & shellfish officer for the Town of Wareham for over 45 years. He continued to work in that position even after his diagnosis at age 84 until the age of 87, and he never missed a day of work while under going many rounds of chemotherapy and radiation. His fellow officers called him invincible.He was the devoted and loving husband of Evelyn Shirley (Collins) for 57 years,a lifelong resident of  18 Hope St., Taunton and a parishioner of St. Mary’s Church. James was a summer resident of Swifts’ Beach, Wareham, MA for the past 57 years and he was the son and only child of the late Joseph P. and Cora A. (Daley).He leaves his two daughters Deborah A. Moore of Holliston, MA, her husband, John D. and his grandson & “buddy” John J., his daughter Karen A. Baptista and her daughter Lauren, “the apple of his eye”, of Dover, MA. He is also survived by Louise Collins a sister-in-law and Robert Collins, a brother-in-law, and many nieces and nephews. He attended Coyle High School and graduated from Taunton High School, Class of 1938. James played baseball and basketball while in high school.  He proudly served his country as a Private First Class in the Army during WWII and was a past commander of the VFW along with his father (the only father & son to hold that position in Taunton). He marched in the Drum & Bugle Corp along with his wife Shirley who was a majorette.He loved to shellfish at Swifts’ Beach and always loved a good clam boil with his wife, family, and friends at his summer home. He was one of the few remaining members of the Bania Calda  Club of Taunton. He loved all sports. This past spring he was a special guest of honor of the CEO of MetroWest Medical Center, Andrei Soran, at a Doctor’s Day event at the Crown Plaza in Natick where he met Doug Flutie, fulfilling a dream. He was Doug Flutie’s most loyal fan from his BC days to the present.He was a solderer, manufacturing sprinkler heads for fire suppression for Hodgman Manufacturing Company and later a foreman following in his father’s footsteps. He then worked for Fernandes Supermarkets at the headquarters in Norton. He also was a Commissioner on the City of Taunton Alcoholic Beverage Commission for many years. He was a Judge for the State at the Raynham and Dighton Race Tracks and later tended bar at both. James also worked in the food service program for the Taunton Public School system. He was always working several jobs.He was very active in politics and one of the most proudest moments of his life was when he was asked to drive the then Sen. John F. Kennedy, running for president, in his Cadillac convertible campaigning in Taunton. Most recently he was very proud to serve as his grandson’s sponsor as his grandson received his Confirmation. His grandson wrote about him, “My grandfather is a good sponsor because he has high spirits all the time, in sickness or health. He is very optimistic and inspiring. He never loses hope, which is the greatest inspiration.”Visiting hours are Friday, Dec. 28, from 5 to 8 PM at O’Keefe-Wade Funeral Home, 70 Washington St., Taunton, MA 02780. A funeral will be held from the O’Keefe-Wade Funeral Home at 8 AM, Saturday, Dec. 29 followed by a funeral mass at 9 AM at St. Mary’s Church, corner of Broadway & Washington Sts., Taunton, MA. Expressions of sympathy maybe made to the Cancer Care Center, MetroWest Medical Center, 115 Lincoln St., Framingham, MA 01702-9167, attention the James F. Baptista Memorial Fund.

Colonel Anthony D. Potter, 71, of 99 US Route 2, Randolph, NH passed away suddenly on December 22, 2007 at his home. He was born in Gorham, NH on March 23, 1936 the son of Dean S. and Verna (Coolidge) Potter and was a graduate of Gorham High School with the Class of 1954. He graduated with a B.S. in Mechanical Engineering from UNH in 1958 and was a graduate of the ROTC Program at UNH.  He entered the military and attained the rank of full Colonel.  He served 27 years in the US Army with many assignments including ROTC Instructor at UNH, two tours in Korea and one tour in Vietnam with the special forces in Ban Mi Tho (Highlands in Vietnam). Tony attended the US Army War College in Carlisle, PA and was selected leader of his class. From 1976 to 1979 he served as Commander of the United Nations Truce Supervision Organization (UNTSO) in the Middle East, mostly in Lebanon. He was Post Commander at Fort Devens, MA and retired from the service in 1985. He taught advanced math at Milford High School and lived in New Boston for 15 years and moved to Randolph in 1998.  Tony was an avid salt water fly fisherman and enjoyed surf flycasting on the Maine Coast for strippers and blue fish. He also enjoyed fly fishing in area brooks and streams. He loved his children and grandchildren, watching the wildlife in the area and enjoyed mowing lawns, shoveling snow and cutting wood. Members of the family include two daughters Elizabeth Marie Potter and her husband Wells Hodous of Round Pond, ME and Jennifer Dean Potter and her husband Aric McAllister of Seattle, Washington; a son Dean Spaulding Potter and his wife Stephanie Davis of Moab, Utah and Yosemite, California; five grandchildren Oliver Hodous, Avery Hodous, Dorothy Hodous, Cyrus Potter-McAllister and Dahlia Potter-McAllister; two sisters Elizabeth Drew and her husband Edward of Gorham and Linda Gordon of Jefferson; a brother Robert Potter and his wife Roberta Arbree of Randolph; nieces, nephews and cousins. There will be no services or calling hours. Anyone who wishes may make a donation to the Non Game and Endangered Wildlife Program, NH Fish & Game Dept., 11 Hazen Drive, Concord, NH 03301 in his memory. Arrangements are by the Bryant Funeral Home.

Constance M. Bureau, 71 of Nashua, NH died December 22, 2007 at the Huntington of Nashua. Connie was born in Chicago, IL on August 18, 1936, the daughter of the late John and Sophia (Pacer) Wojtach. She had resided in Nashua for the past three years, formerly living in Derry, NH for more than 40 years. She received her BSN from the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor, MI where she met her husband Edward. Connie briefly practiced nursing in Ann Arbor and Chicago prior to her marriage in 1959. After moving to Derry, she continued nursing at the Bon Secours Hospital in Methuen, MA and the Alexander Eastman Hospital in Derry. Connie, a member of the La Leche League, taught Childbirth Education classes with the Derry Visiting Nurse Association for many years. She took pride that some of her later students were the children of parents she had previously taught. Connie enjoyed gardening, she truly liked every flower, but was particularly proud of her roses. She was a past President of the Derry Garden Club and continued her activity with the club even after moving to Nashua. While living at the Huntington of Nashua, Connie was one of the original members of the Residence Council and served in several committees including Chair of the Outreach Committee. She also enjoyed traveling, especially cruises to the Caribbean and Alaska. Connie will be greatly missed by her friends in Derry and at the Huntington of Nashua.  She is survived by her husband of 48 years, Edward D. Bureau II of Nashua; one son, Edward D. Bureau III and his wife Susan of Bedford; four daughters, Deborah LaFreniere and her husband Gary of N. Swanzey; Susan Rodriguez and her husband Imar of San Diego, CA; Nancy Bureau-Newman and her husband Forrest of Ft. Collins, CO; and Sarah Garvin and her husband Patrick of Derry. Connie also leaves behind seven beloved grandchildren, Hannah and Michael Bureau, Joelle LaFreniere, Camilla and Isabella Rodriguez and Zachary and Benjamin Garvin.  Following cremation, calling hours will be held on Friday, December 28, 2007 from 5 – 8pm at the Peabody Funeral Home, 15 Birch St., Derry. A mass of Christian burial will be celebrated on Saturday, December 29, 2007 at 10:00am in the Immaculate Conception Church, 216 E. Dunstable Rd., Nashua. Burial will be at a later date in Rollinsford, NH.  In lieu of flowers, memorial contributions may be made to the Huntington Scholarship Fund, Attention Christine Hallock, 55 Kent Lane, Nashua, NH 03062 or to the Derry Garden Club, C/O Laurel Fecteau, 8 Heritage Circle, Hudson, NH 03051.

LEICESTER: James J. Coughlin,55, of 186 River Street, in the Rochdalesection of Leicester, died Saturday, Dec. 22 after a long and courageousbattle with cancer, surrounded by the love of his family and friends.Jim leaves his high school sweet heart, his sole mate, his wife  of 36years, Aleta I. (Cutler) Coughlin, two sons Brad J. Coughlin of Worcesterand Todd M. Coughlin of No. Oxford, a grandson Chad James Coughlin ofSouthbridge, and his brother Daniel Coughlin and his wife Faye of SanAntonio, Tx.Born in Worcester, he was the son of Daniel E. and Claire A. (Perry)Coughlin, III. He grew up in Leicester and graduated from Leicester High in1969. Shortly after High School, he started his career with the LeicesterHighway Dept., first as a mechanic, then as foreman and finally in 1983becoming the youngest Highway Superintendent in Massachusetts at age 32.During his career, Jim was treasurer of the Mass. Highway Assoc., served onthe committee to build the new Police Station, worked to help renovate theRochdale Athletic Field, and was involved in many community projects.Jim possessed a true love of life and his interests and hobbies wereendless. He was a member of the Leicester Rod and Gun Club and the NRA. Jimloved to hunt both birds and deer and especially enjoyed fishing at StilesReservoir where he grew up and then on to the Quabbin, Lake Winnepesaukee,Lake George, Maine, and Onset where he had his boat the "Elmer Fud", andfinally as a traveler in Biloxi, Miss., and Key West, Fla. He was a memberof the Leicester Golf League, Leicester Snowmobile Club and the LeicesterTourquers Car Club. Jim restored a 1955 Chevy which he proudly displayed atthe Summer Nationals in Worcester. He loved vacations with his family andfriends, camping, road trips and Old Orchard Beach. Chief Master Sergeant Esther F. MacKay, US Air Force, retired, the oldest living Air Force Chief Master Sergeant, died on Friday, December 21st at her home. She was 102 years old. Born in Plainville MA, Esther grew up in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia where the only opportunity for major employment was to attend Teachers’ Training. Miss MacKay went on to teach in rural one room schoolhouses in Cape Breton. In 1930 Esther returned to the United States to seek permanent employment.  Esther graduated from the banford hairdressing Academy,in Boston in 1938. In 1943 she enlisted in the Women’s Army Auxiliary Corp to do her part in the defense of her country. She was deployed in England and France and did a second tour in Germany. Esther was in London on the day World War II ended. She was transferred to the Air Force in 1948 and was relocated to Westover AFB in Massachusetts. In 1949 she was called to the Pentagon, working in the Immediate Office of the Secretary of Defense. In 1957 Chief MacKay was sent to NORAD Command Headquarters. She retired in 1965. Chief MacKay had been named an Honorary Charter Chief by the Charter Chiefs Group Board of Directors and was inducted into the organization on August 10 of this year. She was only the third woman to receive this honor. During her years of service Chief MacKay was awarded several awards and decorations including, Air Force Good Conduct Medal, Good Conduct Medal with two silver loops, Women’s Army Corps Service Medal, American Campaign Medal, European-African-Middle Eastern Campaign Medal, World War II Victory Medal (Germany), National Defense Service Medal, Air Force Longevity Service Award Ribbon with four bronze oak leaf clusters, USAF NCO Academy Graduate Ribbon and Joint Commendation Medal. Dear daughter of the late Daniel D. and Collina (MacDonald) MacKay. Loving sister of the late Jessie M. Kachianes and William A. MacKay.  Aunt of Colene Dodsworth and her husband Merle of PEI, Canada. Great Aunt of David Dodsworth and his wife Jane of Medway and Jerrilyn Rinaldi and her husband David of North Reading. Also survived by 4 great great nephews and one great great great niece.  Visiting hours will be held in the Eaton Funeral Home 1351 Highland Ave, Needham on Friday, Jan. 4, 2008 from   4-7 PM, followed by a Memorial Service with Military Honors at 7 PM. Relatives and friends are kindly invited to attend. Interment will take place in the MacKay Cemetery in Cape Breton, Nova Scotia. In lieu of flowers donations in Esther’s name may be made to the New England Shelter for Homeless Veterans, 17 Court St., Boston MA 02118 or to the MacKay Cemetery Association RR#2, West Bay, Nova Scotia BOE 3KO Canada.

M. Lilianne (St. Hilaire) Sousa, age 85, a longtime resident of Dracut, died Friday, Dec. 21 at her home in Dracut. Robert J. “Bob” Jobin, 92, of Manchester, died December 21, 2007, at St. Teresa Health Care Center after a period of declining health. He was born in Manchester, August 1, 1915, and was the son of Elzear and Marie-Anne (Duhamel) Jobin. He was a lifelong resident of Manchester. Mr. Jobin worked as a sheet metal foreman for AW Therrien Roofing Co. for 50 years. He served as a Manchester Highway Commissioner for 30 years. He also served on the Manchester School Board and as a cemetery trustee. He was a member of the St. Vincent de Paul Society, the League of a 1000 Men of the Precious Blood Monastery, and a member of the Knights of Columbus. He was also on the board of directors of L’Union St. Jean Baptiste. Mr. Jobin was a longtime communicant of the former St. Therese Church where he served as a Eucharistic Minister. He was also captain of the 300 Club of St. Therese Parish. He was presently a communicant of Blessed Sacrament Church.  He worked with Holy Cross bingo for 30 years and was instrumental in building Rosary Hall at Villa Augustina. He was predeceased by his wife of 66 years, Lucienne “Lou” (Bourassa) Jobin, who died in 2003, and by a daughter, Diane Jobin, who died in 1947. Family members include two sons, Donald Jobin, of Manchester, and Richard Jobin, of Attleboro, MA; a daughter, Mrs. Maurice (Gloria) Pilotte, of Manchester; 12 grandchildren; 9 great grandchildren; many nieces and nephews.   A Mass of Christian Burial was celebrated Friday December 28, 2007 at Blessed Sacrament Church. Burial followed in St. Augustin Cemetery. Memorial donations may be made to the new chapel of St. Teresa Health Care Center, 519 Bridge St., Manchester, NH 03104

DRACUT - Wilfred R. Levasseur, aged 84, beloved husband, treasured father, and friend to many, died unexpectedly on December 21st from a fall in his home.   Born in Dover, NH on August 28, 1923.  He was the son of the late Alfred and Emilie (Dumont) Levasseur. Will attended Lowell High School and St. Anslem's College.  He served three years in the United States Navy during World War II. Mr. Levasseur was the owner of Cote's Market in Lowell until he retired in 1983, but still dropped by to lend a helping hand on an almost daily basis. In his free time he enjoyed gardening.   He was a member of the Massachusetts Grocers Association and the Lowell Grocers Association, where he served eight years as secretary and treasurer.  He was also a member of the C.M.A.C., and the Knights of Columbus.   For the past several years, Will has been involved with the Dracut Senior Citizens and was currently the vice-president.   In addition to his wife of 63 years Gertrude (Cote) Levasseur is survived by two daughters Diane Tardiff and her husband William of New Ipswich, NH and Lowell and Carmen DeLuca and her husband Henry of Pelham, NH: a son Roger Levasseur and his wife Roberta of Pelham, NH; four sisters Simonne Baribeault of Lowell, wife of the late Roger Baribeault, Pauline Cox and her husband  Donald of Dracut, Cecile Demers and her husband Maurice of Hudson, NH, and Evelyn Bakalyar, and her husband Robert of Dracut; five grandchildren Bradford DeLuca, Nicole Tardiff, Kerrie (Levasseur) Krawczyk, and Kurt and Justin Levasseur; and three great grandchildren, Olivia and Sophia DeLuca, and William Gallagher. Friends may call at the DRACUT FUNERAL HOME, 2159 Lakeview Avenue, DRACUT, on Thursday from 3 until 7PM.  His funeral will be held from the funeral home on Friday at 9AM followed by his Funeral Mass to be celebrated at 10AM at St. Mary Magdalen Church in Dracut.  Interment in St. Joseph Cemetery in Chelmsford.

Harold Alphee Gagnon, 96, formerly of Gilford and Gilmanton, died peacefully Friday morning, December 21, 2007 at the Glenncliff Home, Glencliff, New Hampshire. Born in Manchester, New Hampshire on October 11, 1911 where he was raised and schooled, Harold was the son of the late Thomas and Ophelia (Bonneau) Gagnon.  A home builder in Manchester for several years, Harold moved his family to Gilford in the late 1940’s and founded Silver Sands Motel & Marina where he had dredged the brook to become a channel in from Lake Winnipesaukee to what is now the motel and marina. He dug by hand and set the footings and side walls for the motel and marina using his wheelbarrow and shovel. He also built all of his own homes as well as many others in Gilford, and Gilmanton with his unique fieldstone fireplaces. He was instrumental in the development of the former Mt. Rowe Ski Area in Gilford, and was one of the first men in New Hampshire to make man made snow. Later in life with his love for horses, he moved to Gilmanton on Route 106 where he built another home and a horse arena. There he boarded and trained horses, as well as giving riding lessons. Harold had great pride in showing his Arabians in many horse shows, one particular show he was Grand Champion with his colt Sir Romson.   Harold often spent summers in Wells and Ogunquit, Maine with his family. He loved to ski, dance, country music, roller skating, musicals, and hard work. Harold is predeceased by his first wife, Julia (Tullgren) Gagnon; and a son, Bibe Gagnon in 2005. Harold is survived by a son, Garrett Gagnon and his wife Jennifer of Loudon; a daughter, Marlene Osborne and her husband Paul of Gilmanton; two step-sons, James Mastine and his wife Laureen of Northfield, and Bruce Mastine and his wife Kelly of Bethel, Vermont; ten grandchildren, Chris Gagnon, Lisa Osborne, Nikolas, Kelsea, and Alisha Gagnon, Stephen, Katelyn, Courtney, Amanda, and Jessie Mastine; three great-grandchildren, Noland, and Kaitryn Gagnon, and Lily Mastine. Myra Little his former wife of Virginia also survives Harold. Visiting hours were held on Friday, January 11, 2008 from 5:00-8:00 p.m. at the Simoneau-Paquette Memorial Home, 139 Church Street, Laconia. A private graveside service will be held at Pine Grove Cemetery, Belknap Mountain Road, Gilford, in the spring.  For those who wish, the family suggests expressions of sympathy in Harold’s name be made to Live and Let Live Farm (a non-profit rescue shelter for horses) 20 Paradise Lane, Chichester, NH 03258, or visit www.liveandletlivefarm.org

SALEM - Elaine M. Pelletier, 56, of Salem, died Friday morning, December 21, 2007 at the Kaplan Family Hospice House, Danvers, following a long illness. Born in Salem, she was the daughter of the late Arthur J. & Alice (Berube) Pelletier. A lifelong resident of the city, she was a graduate of St. James High School in the Class of 1969. Elaine was currently employed at Specialized Receivables, Inc., in Wakefield. Russell G. Boudreau, Sr., 91 Dateline: Norton, MA      Russell G. Boudreau, Sr., 91, a lifelong resident of Norton died on Friday December 21, 2007 at the Sturdy Memorial Hospital in Attleboro, MA. He was the beloved husband of Dorothy (Sylvia) Boudreau.     Born in Chester, Penn. on March 28, 1916 he was a son of the late George and Beatrice (Worthley) Boudreau     He grew up in Norton and was a1934 graduate of Norton High School. He served in the United States Army during WWII and was honorably discharged in 1946 with the rank of Technician Third Grade.     Mr. Boudreau was a retired Calibrator having worked for the Foxboro Co. in Foxboro for over 25 years prior to retiring. He had formerly been employed at the former Talbot’s Woolen Mill in Norton where he was a supervisor.     A member of the Trinitarian Congregational Church in Norton Russell was also a member of both the Norton VFW Post 8049 and the Lieutenant Harold A. Healy American Legion Post 222. He was a member of the Foxboro Co. 10 year and 25 year clubs     He was a former coach for the Norton Little League Association and when younger was a pitcher for the AAA Baseball Team in Attleboro where he received the nickname of “Lefty” for his exceptional pitching skills, he also enjoyed bowling but most of all spending time with his loving family and attending his grandson’s baseball games were his greatest pleasures.     Besides his beloved wife he is survived by his devoted children; Alice M. Boudreau and Russell G. “Butch” Boudreau, Jr. and his wife Ann all of Norton, his cherished grandchildren; Joseph Poirier and Stephen Poirier both of Mansfield and Rachel Boudreau, Melody Boudreau and Russell George Boudreau, III all of Norton.     Private funeral services will be held on Monday December 24th at the Norton Memorial Funeral Home 19 Clapp St.(Off Route 140, Taunton Ave.) Norton. Burial will follow in the Norton Common Cemetery, Norton.     Visiting hours have been omitted.     For additional information please visit www.nortonmemorial.com

Dorothy Jeannette Gath...Tewksbury quilter; age 88TEWKSBURY - Dorothy Jeannette (Garrigan) Gath, age 88, a senior member of a large and well known family, died peacefully on Thursday evening, December 20, at her Tewksbury home, surrounded by her loving family. She worked for several years at the former King's Department Store at the Stadium Plaza in Tewksbury, and worked her entire life as a seamstress and tailor.  She was a member of the Tewksbury Quilting Club, the "Piecemakers."Mrs. Gath was a resident of  Tewksbury for sixty eight years, and had been a communicant of St. William's Church for nearly seven decades.She is survived by two daughters and their spouses, Mary Jane and Thomas Bartolone of Tewksbury, and Charlene and James Sullivan of Ft. Myers, FL;  four sons and their spouses, David and Marjorie (Stewart) Gath of Tewksbury, Charles O. Gath Jr., of Meredith, NH, John and Lorelei (Bean) Gath of Billerica, Joseph and Debra (Tontodonato) Gath of Tewksbury; sixteen grandchildren; twenty three great grandchildren;  a sister, Mary Grangel of PA; her sisters-in-law, Gladys Garrigan of Chelmsford, Alice Pollinger of Lowell, Louise (Marion) Gath of Deering, NH, Lynnette Gath and  Edith Gath both of Tewksbury;  a brother-in-law, Lucien Monbleau of Tewksbury; and numerous nieces and nephews.She was also sister of the late William, David, John, George, and Edward Garrigan, Josephine Boisvert, and Viola Monbleau. Her Funeral Mass was celebrated Thursday, Dec. 27,  in St. William's Church,Tewksbury. CHARLTON- Walter G. Daubney, 85, died Thursday, December 20, 2007 in his home after a brief illness.  His wife, Elaine M. (Shea) Daubney died in 2003.  He leaves five children, Diane Sullivan and husband Raymond of Charlton, Donna Bigelow and husband Brian of Charlton, Barbara Karman and husband Sid of Shrewsbury, Richard Daubney of CT and James Daubney and wife Rosemary of Charlton.  In addition, he leaves fourteen grandchildren, thirty-six great grandchildren, a brother Kenneth Daubney in OK, a sister Marjorie Knight of Millbury and many nephews and nieces.   He was born in Douglas on November 6, 1922 to James and Marjorie (Turner) Daubney.  In 1946, Walter married his sweetheart Elaine Shea, and together they shared 57 years of beautiful love and happiness.  The greatest accomplishment and joy to Walter and Elaine was their family.  During his lifetime, Walter developed varied interests, which took him to a forty-year career as a machinist at Whitin Machine Works.  He also served as a part-time patrolman in the Uxbridge Police Department.  In his spare time he did cabinetry and woodworking.  During World War II, he served his country as a radio operator and bombardier on a B17 bomber in the 100th Bomb Group flying out of England over the European continent.  For this turbulent time in the Army Air Corp, Walter was awarded the Victory Medal, the Bronze Air Medal GO 1008 HQ3rd Air Div., Good Conduct Medal and American Theater Campaign Ribbon.  He was a lifetime member of the VFW Post# 1385 in Uxbridge.   Together, Walter and Elaine worked as a team to establish and successfully operate Applewood Campgrounds of Charlton for over 25 years.  Along the way they made many dear friends in their endeavors as campground owners and square dancers.  Walter’s most recent joy was developing a nature sanctuary with two miles of walking trails and picnic area.  He hoped to open this sanctuary for the public’s enjoyment. Visiting hours will be on Wednesday, Dec. 26th from 2-4 PM and 6-8 PM in Callahan & Fay Brothers Funeral Home 61 Myrtle St., Worcester.  Funeral services will be held at 11:00 AM on Thursday, Dec. 27th in the funeral home.  Interment will follow in St. John’s Cemetery, Worcester.  In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to the UMASS Memorial Hospice, UMASS Memorial Foundation, P.O. Box 2795, Worc., MA 01613-9938 or the Cystic Fibrosis Foundation, 220 North Main St., Suite 104, Natick, MA 01760.  For directions and online condolence book please visit www.callahanfay.com

A memorial service remembering Elizabeth Erwin Burnside has been set for 2:00 p.m. on Saturday, January 12, 2008, at the Ridge Avenue Church of Christ at 1625 Ridge Avenue in Tifton.  John Voss will officiate.  Mrs. Burnside, 84, known to many as "Burnsy", passed away December 20, 2007, at Tift Regional Medical Center. The seventh of eight children, she was born at home to Daniel Tucker Erwin and Clara Belle Mann Erwin, both deceased, on August 13, 1923, in Tampa, Florida.  She graduated from Tampa's Plant High School in January 1941, then earned her bachelor's degree at the University of Tampa in June 1944, double-majoring in history and English. She married former Tampa resident and high school classmate, Joseph Edgar Burnside, Jr. in June 1944, in Santa Ana, California, during his service in the United States Marine Corps.  Her husband of more than 50 years, he preceded her in death. After World War II, the couple returned to Gainesville, Florida, where Mrs. Burnside pursued her education, soon earning a master's degree in English.  After they relocated to Madison, Wisconsin, she began a teaching career, which would see her make many friends among faculty and students alike. In 1952, the couple moved to Tifton, and "Burnsy" continued her service as an educator, teaching English at Tift County High School. After a move from Tifton to Carbondale, Illinois, in 1955, and serving as a mother and homemaker, she continued her career as an educator, working as a substitute teacher in that community's public school system.  She earned her real estate license and began what also became a successful career in that field. Returning to Tifton in 1970, she again directed her energies and leadership to education, initially serving as a substitute teacher and guidance counselor at what was then the Len Lastinger Elementary School.  Soon, she was elevated to principal at Charles Spencer Elementary School, where she served until retiring. During her working career and in retirement, she received numerous awards and recognitions.  She also served as a leader in many service organizations, including the Delta Zeta sorority, the Pilot Club and the Red Hat Ladies, to name only a few. "Burnsy" is survived by one son, Joseph E. "Jeb" Burnside, III, of Sarasota, Florida; one sister, Alma DeWald of Tampa, Florida; two grandchildren, Jeremy Burnside and Kelsea Burnside, both of Alexandria, Virginia; and many former students, colleagues, friends and loved ones. Her family welcomes all those who remember her to join them at the memorial on Saturday, January 12, 2008. In lieu of flowers, donations in her memory may be made to the Raintree Village Children's Home in Valdosta.

Arthur Paul Libman, a lifelong resident of Chelsea, entered into rest on December 20, 2007 at the Kaplan Family Hospice House in Danvers.  He was 81 years old. 	Born in Boston, the son of the late Harry & Mary (Harmon) Libman, Arthur was raised & educated in Chelsea.  He enlisted in the United States Navy on November 5, 1943 in Boston.  He began his service at the United States Naval Training School in Newport, R.I. & then served aboard the USS Decker,  the USS Y.O.222, & at the Mare Shipyard at Mare Island, CA.  He earned the rank of Quartermaster 2nd Class, & was the recipient of the World War II Victory Medal, the American Area Ribbon, & the European African Middle Eastern Area Ribbon with 1 star.  Arthur was honorably discharged at the USN Personnel Separation Center in Boston on May 12, 1946.  Upon returning from military service, Arthur married his childhood sweetheart, Cynthia Madow, & settled to begin their family in Chelsea.  For a number of years they enjoyed spending winters at their home in North Miami Beach, FL. Arthur & Cynthia shared true love & affection until her death in 1989.  Arthur was a lifelong glazier, & founded Alliance Glass Corporation in Dorchester in 1958.  Arthur was still quite active in the operation of the glass company & was honored to have his son Stephen & grandson Todd join him in the family business.  Sadness struck Arthur again, with the loss of his grandson in 2006.  A lifelong member of Temple Emmanuel in Chelsea, Arthur found comfort in his religion.  He was also a member of the Moses Michael Hayes Lodge of Masons.  Arthur was a kind & gentle man who will be long remembered as a "mensch".  In addition to his love of family & friends, Arthur also enjoyed bowling & golf.  	Arthur is survived by his son, Stephen Libman & his wife Donna of Lynnfield; his daughter, Susan Schraub & her husband James of Hollywood, FL; grandchildren, Stacey Libman, C.J. Schraub, & Samantha Schraub; & his brother Robert Libman & his wife Judy of N. Miami Beach, FL. 	Arthur's funeral will be held on Sunday, December 23, 2007 at 11:00 AM in Temple Emmanuel, 60 Tudor St. Chelsea, MA 02150.  Burial will follow in the Agudos Shalom Cemetery, Fuller St. Everett.  Memorial observance will be private.  Those wishing to remember Arthur may do so through memorial donations to Temple Emmanuel at the above address.  Funeral arrangements entrusted to the care of the Torf Funeral Chapel of Chelsea.

DERRY, NH   Robert F. Cobban, 81, died peacefully surrounded by wife and children on Wednesday, December 19, after a long illness.  For 50 of his 81 years, “Bob” was the loving and well-loved husband of Nancy (Pennington) Cobban.  He was born in Stamford, CT on December 7, 1926 to Margaret (Mitchell) Cobban and Robert F. Cobban Sr., and throughout his childhood was immersed in the customs, music, and dance of Scotland.  Following his graduation from Stamford High School in 1943, Bob joined the US Navy, and served in the Pacific and China until the end of World War II.  	Back in Connecticut, he held a variety of jobs while attending the University of Connecticut, finally settling on a company that specialized in drafting and design.  This job formed the foundation for his later career, which led him to several companies where he worked on the design and production of specialized electronic connectors and later on the design and engineering of complex flexible circuitry.  These skills led to jobs in New Jersey, Pennsylvania, Massachusetts, and finally, New Hampshire where Bob and the family sank deep roots on the shores of Big Island Pond.  He became a very active member of the Hampstead Congregational Church and the music that had been an important part of his childhood now became an avocation. His rich bass voice served as foundation for the harmonies of many local singing groups including the choir of the Hampstead Congregational Church, the Interfaith Choir (Salem), the Manchester Choral Society, and special presentations of the First Congregational Church in Manchester.  	Living in New Hampshire also reconnected Bob with his Scottish heritage, and the annual Highland Games became a regular event for the whole family and made kilts standard in the family closets.  Church amateur nights rang to the comic songs of Harry Lauder, with Bob, kilted and “bonneted” bringing down the house with the Wiggle Waggle o’ his Kilt and other Scottish favorites.  Another special costume that he wore at Christmas for 30 years was a bright red Santa Claus suit.  He delighted and took delight in the joy of hundreds of children sitting on his lap and sharing their secrets with him.As well as his wife, Nancy, Bob is survived by one son, Robert Alexander Cobban and his wife, Rachel, of Derry, daughter, Marion M. Cobban of Chester and her dear friend Linda Pollack, five grandchildren and one great-granddaughter, two sisters, one brother-in-law, and many nieces and nephews living from coast to coast.  He was pre-deceased by his younger daughter, Margaret. 	A memorial service will be held on Saturday, January 19, 2008 at 1:00pm in the Hampstead Congregational Church, Main St., Hampstead.

WESTBOROUGH__Irene R. (Morawski) Niemela 91, formerly of 45 Suomi St., Paxton, died Wednesday December 19, in Beaumont Nursing Center, Westborough.  She was the widow of John J. Niemela who died in 1954.  Irene was born October 5, 1910, in Worcester, daughter of the late Roman and Pelagia (Strzelecki) Morawski.   Irene resided in Paxton for 55 years.  She married John Niemela in St. Mary's Church, Worcester in 1934.  They lived in Worcester for one year, and then moved to Paxton.  Her husband worked at Bell Co., for several years, then at Harrington Richardson Co. for 14 years. She leaves five children, Joan I., wife of the late Daniel Hartley of Attleboro; John J. Niemela, husband of Jane (Collard) of West Rotunda, FL., Dorothy P. Cary of Worcester; Pauline S., wife of Kenneth Warren of Northborough and Mary K. Locke of Houston, TX.; 15 grandchildren, 27 great-grandchildren, 4 great great-grandchildren and many nieces and nephews. She was predeceased by four brothers, Joseph, Carl, Chester and Roman Morawski.   Irene attended Commerce High School and in 1927 during the Great Depression left school at the age of 16 in her junior year to weave at Duffy Manufacturing Co.  She graduated from high school in 1929. After her husband died, she again worked at Duffy Manufacturing Co., Lusignan Textile Co., and Sprague Electric, Inc.  After retiring, she worked as a Senior Aide for the Right to Read Program located at Worcester State College where she coordinated reading materials.  She then transferred to the English as a Second Language Division at Worcester Center where she interviewed volunteers.  She later became very active in the Paxton Senior Center, assisting in the dining room as a volunteer.  She enjoyed crocheting, playing piano, cooking, baking and dancing.  She was a member of both St. Columba Church in Paxton and Our Lady of Czestochowa Church in Worcester.  Funeral services and burial will be private.  HENRY-DIRSA FUNERAL HOME, 33 Ward Street, Worcester is directing funeral arrangements.

Kenneth F. Kane, 84 years of N. Falmouth, formerly of Brockton and N. Easton, died December 19, 2007 at Bourne Manor.  Beloved husband of Margaret A. (Herlihy) Kane to whom he was married for 60 years.  Loving son of the late Timothy F. and Irene (Shea) Kane.  Loving and wonderful father of Kathleen K. Norton and her husband Richard of N. Easton and Pocasset, Paula K. Norton and her husband Jack of N. Easton and Mashpee, Timothy F. Kane of N. Falmouth and the late David F. Kane; grandfather of Elizabeth Norton Woods, Christine K. Norton and Jacqueline M. Norton all of South Boston; devoted brother and best friend of Marilyn K. Buckley of Brockton and brother of the late Franklin E., Robert A and William D. Kane. Ken graduated from Brockton High School, class of 1940.  He enlisted in the Army Air Force during World War II and was a 2nd lieutenant and navigator of B-17s in the 100th Bomb Group.  His plane, Humpty Dumpty, was shot down over Germany on December 31, 1944.  He survived by parachuting into enemy territory and spent the rest of the war in a German POW camp.  He was released by invading Russian troops at war’s end in May 1945. After the war, Ken was one of the first to graduate from Bentley College.  He worked with the IRS for many years and for several large accounting firms before starting his own accounting practice, Kenneth F. Kane, CPA and Associates. A member of Thorny Lea Golf Club since the age of 13, following the tradition established by his father, an original member of the club, Ken had a passion for golf and accomplished the amazing by shooting four holes-in-one throughout his golfing career. Ken was also a pioneer patient for coronary by-pass surgery and survived three triple bypasses from 1975 on.  He was a very agreeable man, a gentleman in every respect and will be sorely missed by family and friends.   Funeral from the Conley Funeral Home, 138 Belmont Street (Rte 123) Brockton Saturday at 8am.  Funeral Mass in Our Lady of Lourdes Church at 9am.  Burial will be held Monday in the Massachusetts National Cemetery, Bourne.  Visiting Hours Friday 4-8pm.  In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to the Parkinson’s Disease Foundation, 1359 Broadway, Suite 1509, New York, NY  10018, for online donations click here

New Bedford - Gerald G. Delano, Sr., 83, of N. Dartmouth passed away Wednesday, December 19, 2007 at St. Luke’s Hospital after a long illness.  He was the husband of the late Agnes O. (Broadland) Delano. Born in Fall River, son of the late Joseph K. and Dora W. (Wilbur) Delano, he was a lifelong resident of Dartmouth.  A graduate of Dartmouth High School Class of 1942, he also attended New Bedford Vocational School.  Prior to entering the Army in 1943, he worked at the Boston Navy Yard.  A veteran of WWII, he served his country in the United States Army as a supply sergeant from 1943 - 1946, serving overseas for 28 months.  He was the owner / operator of Joseph K. Delano Saw Mill, Inc.  Mr. Delano was a member of the Smith Mills Christian Congregational Church in Dartmouth and was also a long time Registrar of Voters and a Town Meeting Member for the Town of Dartmouth. Mr. Delano entered Masonry by joining Noquochoke Lodge A.F. & A.M. in Westport in 1958. That same year he became a life member of Noquochoke Lodge A.F. & A.M. and in 1965 he served as Worshipful Master.  He was the District Deputy Grand secretary from 1967 - 1968.  In 1969 he served as Grand Pursuivant of the Grand Lodge of MA and became the District Deputy Grand Master of the New Bedford 30th Masonic District where he served for 2 years: 1973 & 1974.  He affiliated with Abraham H. Howland, Jr. Lodge in 1975 and was a Grand Representative of the Grand Lodge of MA and was recipient of the Joseph Warren Medal in 1980.  Mr. Delano served as Deputy Grand Master , became a permanent member, and received the Henry Price Medal of the Grand Lodge of MA through Noquochoke Lodge A.F. & A.M. in 1984.  He was a member of the Aleppo Temple A.A.O.N.M.S. of Boston and a member of the Southeastern Shrine Club.   He joined the Scottish Rite Valley of Fall River in 1972 and was their treasurer from 1985 - 1999; and was appointed assistant treasurer in 2000 and still holds that position.  He was bestowed the highest rank of the order, 33rd Degree in 1993 by the Supreme Council.  Mr. Delano joined the York Rites in New Bedford in 1959 and was treasurer of the Council for 5 years starting in 1961 and treasurer of the Commandery for 8 years starting in 1962.  He was initiated into the Order of the Eastern Star by Cortez Allen Chapter # 161 in Westport in 1960 and was Worthy Patron in 1967.  Survivors include 2 sons: Gerald G. Delano, Jr. and his wife Veronica of N. Dartmouth and John K. Delano, Sr. and his wife Cynthia of Fairhaven; a brother: Arthur J. Delano and his wife Barbara of Erie, PA; 2 sisters: Orina Reagan of N. Dartmouth and Claire Driesen and her husband Donald of N. Dartmouth; 5 grandchildren: John Kempton, Jr. of Los Angeles, CA, Jillian Kate Delano of Fairhaven, Elizabeth Grace Delano-Dykas and her husband Adam of Fairhaven, Ellen Marie Delano of Dartmouth and Julie Ann Delano of Dartmouth; and several nieces and nephews. He was brother of the late Wilbur W. Delano and Ann Frias.Funeral to which relatives and friends are invited will be Saturday, December 22, 2007 at the Potter Funeral Home at 10:00 A.M.Calling hours Friday 4 - 8 P.M. with a Masonic Service under the direction of the Noquochoke Lodge A.F. & A.M. at 7:00 PM.  Interment in Beech Grove Cemetery, Westport. 	In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to a charity of your choice.

Marinette R. Beckwith of Yarmouthport, wife of Oliver P. Beckwith for 66 years, and a resident of Yarmouthport, died on December 18th at the age of 93 after a brief illness. Mrs. Beckwith was born in Detroit, MI, grew up in Hastings, NY, and lived for 48 years in Marshfield. During a life dedicated to family and community, she served for years as librarian at the Clift Rogers Library in Marshfield Hills and at Silver Lake High School in Pembroke. She volunteered her time for many different causes, including the League of Women Voters, the Marshfield school system, the Save the Dwyer Farm committee, and for 28 years at Jordan Hospital in Plymouth. She discovered one of the core elements in the lives of all her family when she brought them to Corn Hill in Truro for a summer vacation during WWII, where the Beckwith’s met their life-long closest friends, the family of Tom and Dee Dee O'Neil. There, four generations of the Beckwith’s and O’Neil’s have happily and faithfully reconnected each summer for the past 62 years. In addition to her husband, she is survived by her four children Stephen A. of Providence, RI, John O. and his wife Marion of Marshfield, Marianne of Scotts Valley, CA and Paul G. of San Francisco, CA; by her grandchildren Bryan (who has just returned from Iraq) and wife Leslie, Sarah and husband Jeff, Oliver, Olivia and Henry; and by her three great-grandchildren Madeline, John and Ainsley. A Celebration will be held on Thursday, December 27th at the North Community Church in Marshfield Hills at 12:00 noon. In lieu of flowers, memorial donations may be made to National Multiple Sclerosis Society, P.O. Box 4527, N.Y., NY 10163.

WORCESTER____Miss Florence B. Dusak, 97, of Worcester died Tuesday December 18, 2007 in Holy Trinity Nursing Center.Miss Dusak a former Office Manager at Universal-Cyclops Specialty Steel Division, retired in 1975 after 29 years of service. She went to Universal Cyclops after 17 years as secretary at the Wickwire Spencer Steel Company that relocated to Denver, Colorado.Miss Dusak leaves several nephews and nieces. She was predeceased by seven sisters and one brother.  She was born in Worcester, a daughter of Joseph and Louise (Gut) Dusak and lived here all her life.Miss Dusak was a member of Our Lady of Fatima Church and served on its Parish Council and was a member and served a term as president of its Woman’s Club. She was a member of the Worcester Business & Professional Woman’s Club, The Massachusetts Citizen’s Rights Association, Inc., organized to counteract legislation to fluoridate waters, she was a member of the Natural Foods Association and a life member of the National Health Federation, dedicated to the protection of health freedoms. She was also a member of the Worcester County Mechanics Association and of the American Association of Retired Persons.The funeral will be held on Saturday December 22, 2007 with a Mass at 10:30 a.m. in Our Lady of Fatima Church, 43 Belmont Street. Burial will be in St. John’s Cemetery. A calling period will be held from 10:00 a.m. until the time of the Mass Saturday in Our Lady of Fatima Church. The family requests that flowers be omitted. Memorial contributions may be made to Our Lady of Fatima Parish, 7 Auburn Street, Worcester, MA., 01605. HENRY-DIRSA FUNERAL HOME, 33 Ward Street, is directing funeral arrangements.

She had also volunteered for Stage One at the Palace Theater.  She was a former member of St. Luke & St. Margaret Episcopal Church in Allston, MA.  She will be remembered for her adventurous spirit and dedication to her family.	In her life:  Members of her family include one sister, Ruth Watts of Derry, NH; nephews, Paul Hewitt, Denis Hewitt, Mark Hewitt, Bobby Dion, Kevin Dion, and Thomas McCarthy; nieces, Debbie Dion, Robin Dion, Laurie Lane, Brenda Lane, and Sandy Moore.	She was predeceased by brothers, Jerry Hewitt, Andrew Hewitt, Bob Hewitt, and Henry Hewitt; one sister, Lillian McCarthy, and one niece, Harriet E. Hewitt.	  The funeral service took place in the funeral home chapel on Saturday December 22, 2007 with the Rev. Doug Rickard officiating.  Burial was in Pine Grove Cemetery.	In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to the Salvation Army, 121 Cedar St, Manchester, NH, 03101.

Kathleen M. Grogan, 62 Dateline: Norwood, MA      Kathleen M. (Murphy) Grogan, 62, of Norwood formerly of Norton died on Tuesday December 18, 2007 at New England Sinai Hospital in Stoughton, MA. She was the beloved wife of the late Richard H. “Dick” Grogan who passed away on January 17, 1999.     Born in Norwood, MA on July 4, 1945 she was a loving daughter of Dorothy A. (McDavitt) Murphy of Norwood and the late Dennis J. Murphy, Sr.     She grew up in Norwood, attended St. Catherine’s school and was a graduate of Norwood High School. Mrs. Grogan had worked as a Customer Service Specialist for Stop & Shop in both Franklin and Hyde Park for 40 years prior to retirement.     A former communicant of St. Mary’s Church in Norton, Kathleen enjoyed traveling with friends, gardening in her yard, spending time with her cherished family and had a special place in her heart for dogs.     She had lived in Norton for 33 years before returning to her native hometown of Norwood 2 years ago.     In addition to her mother she is survived by her devoted children; Richard F. Grogan of Boston and Jill A. Grogan of Wrentham, her dear siblings; Maureen Martinho of Norwood, Deborah Pellegrini of Amesbury, Sheila Monaghan of Norwood, David Murphy of Norwood, Denise Allan of Norwood, Michael Murphy of Norwood and the late Diane, Dennis, Jr., Daniel and Mark Murphy, also surviving are many nieces and nephews and her best friend Aussie.      Her funeral, to which relatives and friends are cordially invited to attend, will be held on Saturday December 22nd at 8:30 A.M. from the Sherman & Jackson Funeral home 55 North Main St. Mansfield followed a Mass of Christian burial celebrated at 10:00 A.M. in St. Mary’s Church, South Worcester St, Norton. Burial will follow in the Norton Common Cemetery, Norton.     Visiting hours will be held on Friday December 21st from 5-8 P.M. at the Sherman & Jackson Funeral Home.     Following the services on Saturday all are invited to attend a reception which will be held at the Holiday Inn 31 Hampshire Street in Mansfield.     For additional information please visit www.shermanjackson.com

Buckley, Helene G. (O'Neil) of Hingham died December 18, 2007. Devoted wife of the late Francis J. Buckley.  Beloved mother of Helene G. and her husband James Feeley, Jr. of Hingham, and Ann D. and her husband Michael MacCune of Hingham.  Cherished grandmother of Kristin, Michael and his wife Julie, and James Feeley, III and his wife Diane, and Kim, Sarah, MacKenzie and Michael MacCune, Jr.  Great-grandmother of Kathryn and James Feeley, IV. Born in Paris, France, Helene came to Boston when she was only 6 months old.  She grew up in Boston, but spent many a summer in Hingham with relatives.  Helene married the late Francis Buckley in 1946 in Holy Cross Cathedral, Boston.  Mrs. Buckley enjoyed traveling with her husband and visited such places as Hawaii, Germany, Scotland, Alaska, Mexico, and the Caribbean which was a favorite.  They also enjoyed wintering in West Palm Beach in FL.  Helene and her family resided in Hingham for over 50 years.  She was a clerical worker for the Commonwealth of MA in the Tax Department in Boston for over 10 years.  She enjoyed cooking, gardening, and going to the beach.  Most of all, Helene loved being with her family and spending time with her grandchildren and great-grandchildren. Relatives and friends are respectfully invited to attend the funeral from the Pyne Keohane Funeral Home, 21 Emerald St., HINGHAM, Saturday at 9 AM. Funeral Mass in Saint Paul's Church, Hingham at 10:00 AM. Visiting hours Friday 4 - 8 PM. Burial will be at a later date. In lieu of flowers, donations in memory of Helene G. Buckley may be made to the Children's Hospital, 300 Longwood Ave, Boston, MA 02115.

Donald E. Lane, Sr. was born on January 6, 1957 to Mrs. Ceola Lane and the late Mr. Eugene Lane in San Jose, California.  The LORD called Donald into HIS loving arms as he departed this life on Tuesday, December 18, 2007 at Huntsville, Hospital, Huntsville, Alabama.  Donald's devoted wife did not hear the door that opened and took him beyond her call, for it was a soft as the dropping of a rose petal, but the LORD left Donald's memory to linger with us like the fragrance of a rose, until our own life's door shall close. Donald accepted Christ at an early age.  He attended Ravenswood High School Palo, Alto, California and furthered his education at Stockton Theology Bible College, where he received a degree in Theology.  Donald also served in the United States Marine, where he was honorably discharged. Donald was joined in Holy Matrimony to the former Miss Linda Wright.  Donald's professional career included employment with FMC (United Defense) and JDSU in San Jose, California.  In 2006, he and his wife moved to Huntsville, Alabama and Donald found employment at Alabama A&M University and Verizon as a security guard. Donald was preceded in death by one brother, James Lane and one sister, Violetta McFarland.  Those left to share his legacy of love are: his beloved wife, Linda W. Lane; his loving mother, Ceola A. Lane of East Palo Alto, California; six children:  Donald Lane Jr., Tamra Lane, Paula (Wendell) Parks, Roderick Calloway, Robert (Katie) Calloway, and Ronald (Riba) Calloway; thirteen grandchildren:  Anthony, Johnathan, Whitney, Samuel, Paul, Justin, Jordan, Jada, Eva, Cali, Sara, Claire, and Bernice; three brothers:  Wendell Lane, Wilbert Dean Lane, and Victor Lane; two sisters: Carolyn (Robert) McGrew and Doris Bradford; bother-in-law, William Wright; and a host of nieces, nephews, other relatives and friends. Funeral services for Mr. Donald E. Lane will be held Saturday, December 22, 2007 at 12:00 Noon from the First Church of God and Christ with Pastor Curtis Banks officiating. Interment will be in Valley View Memorial Gardens.

WEBSTER– Theodore R. “Teddy” Hencinski, 83, of 310 Thompson Road, died Tuesday, December 18, in Webster Manor.  He leaves his wife of 58 years, Mary L. (Mangan) Hencinski; a son, James M. Hencinski and his wife Susan of Duluth, MN; a daughter, Jane E. Herrion and her husband James R. of Dudley; a grandson, Christopher J. Herrion of Dudley; and a stepgranddaughter, Ana Lee Bixler of Duluth, MN. His mother, Victoria R. Pincince, predeceased him. He was born in Worcester and lived in Webster and Dudley all his life. He graduated from Bartlett High School in 1942 and completed studies at Worcester Junior College. He was an Army veteran of World War II. Mr. Hencinski co-owned Quality Pizza in Auburn in the 1970's. He worked for several local auto dealerships, retiring from Place Motor in the early 1990's. He was an active member of Saint Anthony of Padua Church in Dudley, serving on many committees; he particularly enjoyed singing tenor in the choir. He was a member and past grand knight of the Knights of Columbus, Webster Council #228, and a member of  the Fourth Degree of the Knights, Cardinal Wright Assembly #1924. He sang in the Greendale Retired Men’s Glee Club for over 10 years. He served the town of Dudley, first as a member of the school committee and then as a selectman. He enjoyed his trips “down south.”       The funeral will be held Saturday, December 22, from the Sitkowski & Malboeuf Funeral Home, 340 School Street, Webster, with a Mass at 10:00 AM in Saint Anthony of Padua Church, 24 Dudley Hill Road, Dudley. Burial will be in Saint Anthony’s Cemetery. Calling hours are from 3:00 to 7:00 PM Friday, Dec. 21, in the funeral home. Donations in his memory may be made to either the Webster Manor Resident/ Activities Fund, 745 School St., Webster, MA 01570 or to the Holy Name Central Catholic High School Music Department, 144 Granite St., Worcester, MA 01604.

Edward Carl Davis, 79, of Fitzgerald died Tuesday, December 18 at his residence surrounded by family and friends. Graveside services will be held 12:00 Friday, December 21 in the Evergreen Cemetery with Rev. Bobby Rodgers officiating.  Mr. Davis was born July 25, 1928 in Ben Hill County to Ruby Stokes Davis and the late J. Edward Davis. He was a 1946 graduate of Fitzgerald High School and attended accounting school in Washington, D.C. Mr. Davis returned to Fitzgerald and went to work for Campbell Coal Company, transferring later to Atlanta. Susan M. (Nicoll) Simoneau, 47, of Manchester died December 18, 2007 at Community Hospice House in Merrimack surrounded by family and loved ones.   Born in Nashua on August 17, 1960, she was the daughter of Robert and Joan (Hawkes) Nicoll.     She grew up in Nashua and began high school at Mt. St. Mary’s.  Her family then moved to Manchester and she graduated from Trinity High School in 1978.  She later earned a degree from the Bauder Fashion College in Florida.   Mrs. Simoneau worked with Designs by Levi’s for over 20 years, starting in sales and moving into management.  She eventually became the designer for Designs by Levi’s stores opening across the country.    The family includes her husband of 25 years, Michael J. Simoneau of Manchester; a daughter, Chelsey E. Simoneau of Manchester; her mother, Joan E. Nicoll of Manchester; her father, Robert E. Nicoll and his wife, Donna of Manchester; a sister, Debra Dunn of Manchester; a brother, Jeffrey J. Nicoll of Holly Springs, NC; aunts, uncles, nieces, nephews and cousins.   IN HER LIFE:  It was during her high school years that she met Mike Simoneau, the man that was to become her husband, best friend and lifelong partner.  In 1992, Sue and Mike’s daughter Chelsey was born and their lives became enriched in ways they could not have imagined.   Upon receiving a diagnosis of breast cancer 12 years ago, she vowed not to let it get the best of her, and it didn’t.  As she always did, Sue displayed a courage that came naturally to her and took on the disease with grace, dignity and determination.  She passed away many years later than expected.   Sue, Mike, Chelsey and all in their family want to express their deep felt gratitude to her friends and neighbors who assisted with her care, stayed with her during the most difficult times, helped the family and mostly for their love.   SERVICES:   A Mass of Christian Burial was celebrated Friday December 21, 2007 at St. Catherine Church, corner of Webster and Hemlock streets, Manchester.  Committal prayers followed at Mt. Calvary Mausoleum, 474 Goffstown Rd., Manchester.   Memorial donations may be made to VNA Hospice, 33 So. Commercial St., Suite 401, Manchester 03101, or to Community Hospice House, 210 Naticook Rd., Merrimack 03054

John J. Joyce...retired from Monsanto, formerly of Tewksbury;  86EFFINGHAM, NH - John Joseph Joyce, a WWII Army Veteran and  former Tewksbury resident, died Tuesday morning, December 18, at the Huggins Hospital in Wolfboro, NH.  He was the husband of Dorothy A. (Joyce) Joyce, who died on April 1, 2007.He was born in Everett, June 30, 1921, a son of the late Charles and Evelyn (McAleer) Joyce, and was raised in Everett.  He interrupted his education to enter into the Army and served in the European Theater just prior to the Allied victory in Europe.  He served out the remainder of his enlistment stateside.  Upon discharge,  he finished his education, and received his high school diploma.He worked his entire career at the Monsanto Chemical company in Everett, and retired as a mechanical engineer.Mr. Joyce lived the greater part of his life in Tewksbury, but moved to the home of his daughter in the Ossippee area of New Hampshire two years ago due to aging issues.He, along with his wife, was an avid horse racing fan, and summered in Scarboro Downs, in Maine.He is survived by three daughters, Jo-Ann Joyce of Tewksbury, Judith Boyce and her husband Henry of N.C., and Janet Hepburn, of Effingham, with whom he resided; a son, John Joyce, and his wife Oko of NY; a sister, Dorothy Mazzapica and her husband Charles of FL; a sister-in-law, Tootsie Joyce of Everett; six grandchildren; four great grandchildren; and many nieces and nephews. His Funeral Service was held Dec. 21,  in the funeral home.  Burial with Army honors was in Tewksbury Cemetery.  Memorials to the St. Jude's Children's Research Hospital, 501 St. Jude Pl., Memphis, TN  38105-1942 will be appreciated.

Mr. Albert J. ‘Gus’ Godin, 71, formerly of 81 Lancaster Road, Gorham, passed away on Monday, December 17th, 2007. He was born in Berlin, on April 24th 1936, the son of Mr. Alexander Godin and Irene Morin. He is survived by his loving wife Jan of 44 years.  He is also survived by five children, sons Daniel Vashaw of Berlin, NH; Tim Godin and his wife Donna of Gorham, NH; and three daughters: Jill Levesque and her husband Rene of Milan, NH; Tina Laflamme and her husband James of Fayetteville, NC and Tammy Moran and her husband Joe of Pembroke, NH. Gus is survived by sisters Lola Bourassa and her husband Florian, of Berlin, NH and Ann Marie Kelly and her husband Gene, of Berlin, NH. Members of his extended family include 12 grandchildren (Eric, Karen, James, Brandon, Shayna, Christopher, Matthew, Taylor, Nicholas, Joshua, Sarah and Alex J) and three great-grandchildren (Brynna, Caitlan and Hannah).    Gus was a well-known and life-long member of the Fraternal Order of Eagles club.  He loved to ride motorcycles, and had served as the director of the Berlin Snowshoe Club. He was a lifelong resident of the Berlin-Gorham area. He started his long career in the heating business at Hodgdon, and then began his own business in Berlin for many years, before retiring to the good life. Summers were spent camping at Israel River campground, where he created “the perfect swing” and entertained his many grandchildren, who loved to call him “Papa” and rock with him into the evening. Gus was playfully known by all his close friends and family as “the man who could do and fix ANYTHING!” Gus will be sorely missed by all who knew him for his quick wit, kind nature and genuine love for all those who surrounded him.  A Mass of Christian Burial will be celebrated on Thursday, December 20th, at 1PM at the St. Anne Church of Good Shepherd Parish in Berlin. Relatives and friends may call at Bryant Funeral Home, 1 Promenade Street in Gorham from 7PM to 9PM on Wednesday, December 19th.  In Memory of Gus and in honor of his grandchild Nicholas, donations may be made to the American Cancer Society, 2 Commerce Drive, Suite 110 Bedford, NH 03110 or to the American Heart Association, 2 Wall Street Manchester, NH 03101.

HERBERT GUSTAV DANIELSON Herbert “Herb” Gustav Danielson, 88, a Whitman icon, died on Monday, December 17.      Born on May 27, 1919, in Boston, Massachusetts, the son of Oscar and Hilma Danielson, Herb lived in Dorchester and Lynn before settling at 459 Washington Street, Whitman.   He was predeceased by his beloved wife of 49 years, Mabel Wilson, his brother Robert and sister Doris.  He is survived by his three sons, Arnold Danielson of Brockton, MA, Robert Danielson of Lynn, MA and Eric Danielson of Corinth, VT, three grandsons, Ian, Kyle and Kelson Danielson; several nieces, nephews and a large extended “adopted” family. Herb was a graduate of Lynn English High School and Hyannis Teachers College and held two Masters Degrees from Boston University.   He served as a Naval Aviator for four years during WWII, achieving the rank of lieutenant.  While serving in Europe during the war he met his future bride, Mabel Wilson.  Herb and Mabel were married in Glasgow, Scotland, in 1946.  They returned to this country to make their home. After teaching physical education and history for 5 years Mr. Danielson became Principal of Whitman Junior High School in 1953 and held that post for 25 years.  After retirement from the school system he served as courier for the Brockton Medical Lab for 10 years.   In 1973 he won the Silver Beaver Award for Distinguished Service to Boyhood, the highest award earned by a civilian from the Boy Scouts of America.  He also won the Patriot Ledger’s “Golden Apple” award in 1995 for dedication and enthusiasm.  He was also an Honorary Member of the Whitman Mothers’ Club.  A member of the First Baptist Church for many years, Herb served as youth director, driving children to swim at the YMCA and acting as lifeguard at church picnics.   The Whitman Board of Selectmen honored Mr. Danielson this year by proclaiming August 19, 2007  “Herbert G. Danielson Day” in recognition for his decades of dedication to the academic and fitness education of the children of Whitman.  The Whitman Recreation Commission declared that from that day forward the hours of 3-4:30 pm daily would be known as ‘The Danielson Buddy Swim’ with a permanent plaque placed at the pool “(to) extend deep gratitude to Herb for his countless contributions to the community.” A private family ceremony will be held at a later date.

Mrs. Bessie Lee Leslie Gideon, better known as “Aunt Bessie” was born on August 3, 1933 to the late Tobe Riley Leslie and Rosie Lee Parker Leslie in Madison County, Alabama. She departed this life on December 17, 2007 at Huntsville Hospital. The Lord saw that the road was getting rough for Aunt Bessie and he needed another flower in the garden so he quietly removed her from this earth. She was preceded in death by her parents, Tobe Riley Leslie and Rosie Lee Parker Leslie; six sisters and three brothers: Margate L. Horton, Martha L. Crawford, Mary L. Gladys Wilson, Aribie L. L. Reed, Louise L. White, Martha L. Burks, Albert Leslie, George Leslie and James Leslie. She accepted Christ in 1956 as her personal Savior and joined the Big Shiloh Church under the leadership of Reverend James Townsend in Madison, Alabama. She sung in the #1 Choir faithfully, until her health failed her. She leaves to cherish her sweet dear memories one devoted and loving sister, Rosanna Hyter (Cleveland); one sister-in-law, Bessie Lee Leslie; two brothers-in-law: James Wilson and John Henry White, both of Huntsville, Alabama; one aunt, Gloria Clemons of Huntsville, Alabama; a devoted cousin, Susie Smith and a special friend, Ernest Pope. Although Aunt Bessie did not have any biological children of her own she had a special place in her heart for all her nieces and nephews. Her doors were always opened whenever anyone needed a place to live or food to eat. She would feed you good and then send food home with you…that was just the kind of person she was. Auntie we are going to miss you but have finished your journey on this side so it’s okay for you to take your rest. Funeral services for will be held on Sunday, December 23, 2007 1:30 p.m. from the Big Shiloh Primitive Baptist Church - Madison, Alabama, with the Elder Woodrow Kirby, Jr. Officiating. Patricia B. Dunn, age 69, of Noank, passed away unexpectedly at her home on Sunday, December 16th, 2007.  She was born on Jan. 12, 1938 in New London, the daughter of Simon Alvah and Eileen Frusher Welt, and lived in Groton until moving with her parents to Mystic in 1952.  Patty attended Groton schools and graduated from Robert E. Fitch High School in 1955, the last class to graduate from the original building. She attended the University of Connecticut and after her graduation taught school in Groton at the former Poquonnock Bridge Elementary School. Patty married David H. Dunn of Stonington, an Electric Boat engineer, in 1960. Eventually the couple settled in Noank where Mrs. Dunn became the director of the Noank Baptist Church nursery school, a post she held for over 20 years.  Following her husband’s death in 1980, Mrs. Dunn was employed by the Bureau of Business Practice in Waterford, and Analysis and Technology in North Stonington, before finally retiring. Mrs. Dunn has been a long-time member of the Noank Historical Society and the Ram Island Yacht Club, as well as being a volunteer instructor in computer technology at the Groton Senior Center. She was especially fond of animals, always owning at least one Springer Spaniel and one or two cats. She was predeceased by her husband in 1980, and her son, David T. Dunn in 2004.  She is survived by her daughter and son-in-law Karen and James Davenport and granddaughters Kristen and Laura, of Ancramdale, New York; her daughter-in-law Kelly Dunn and granddaughters Chelsea and Alyssa, of Jacksonville, Florida; her brother and sister-in-law Robert and Lora Welt of Mystic. She was especially close to her cousin William N. Peterson and her aunt Virginia Niebling. Other surviving cousins are Karen and Wesley Frusher. Mrs. Dunn had several nieces, nephews, grandnieces, and a grandnephew. A Memorial Service will be conducted at the Noank Baptist Church on Friday, December 28th at 3pm. There are no visiting hours.   In lieu of flowers, memorial contributions may be made to the Noank Baptist Church nursery school or, for a charity dedicated to breast cancer research, Susan G. Komen for the Cure, 5005 LBJ Freeway, Ste. 250. Dallas, Texas 75244.  To share a message of remmebrance or condolence with Patty's family, please click the link below.

Herbert L. "Herb" Snyder, 87, of Tifton died Sunday, December 16, 2007, at his residence.  Funeral services will be held at 10:00 a.m. on Tuesday, December 18, 2007, in the Chapel of Bowen-Donaldson Home for Funerals.  Rev. Jimmy Albritton and Dr. Danny V. Ray, Hospice of Tiftarea Chaplain will officiate with burial to follow in Tift Memorial Gardens.  Grandsons will serve as pallbearers.  Honorary pallbearers will be members of the Tifton Police Department and the Tift County Sheriff's Office.  The family will receive friends on Monday, December 17, 2007, from 7:00 to 9:00 p.m. in the Chapel of Bowen-Donaldson Home for Funerals.  The family will also receive guests and friends at 7 Summit Lane in Tifton. Mr. Snyder was born on October 7, 1920 in Washington County and was the son of Cleveland Snyder and Annie Osborne Snyder, both deceased.  He was also preceded in death by one brother, Woodrow Snyder; two sisters, Mattie Mae Brantley and Polly Broome; and a 7-year-old step granddaughter, Aimee Grace.     Mr. Snyder served with the U. S. 8th Air Force during World War II for three years, serving most of that time in England.  He won the World War II Victory Medal, the European-African Middle Eastern Service Medal, the Good Conduct Medal and the AR 600-68 Distinguished Unit Badge.  After returning home, he worked for J. P. Stevens in Baldwin County and, in 1962, relocated to Tifton to J. P. Stevens as a supervisor.  He retired from there and worked for the Tift County Sheriff's Department for 26 years.  He retired from there as a lieutenant in 1992.  He was of the Baptist faith. Mr. Snyder is survived by his wife, Lucille M. Snyder of Tifton; three sons and daughters-in-law, James C. "Jim" and JoAnn Snyder of Tifton, Eugene and Denise Snyder of Cordele and Terry and Brenda Snyder of Turner County; two step-sons and their spouses, Joseph and Tanya Sanders of Pine Mountain and John Lowary of LaGrange; four daughters and sons-in-law, Patsy and Dan Shealy of Winterville, Christine and Jimmy Albritton of Moultrie, Joanne and Tom Burton of Crawford and Mary Nell and Steve Greer of Tifton; one step-daughter, Callie Thornton of Hogansville; four sisters, Ruby Crowe of Milledgeville, Lois Payne of Milledgeville, Ruth Roberson of Gordon and Alma Smallwood of Gordon; 16 grandchildren; and 23 great grandchildren. In lieu of flowers, the family asks for donations to be made to Hospice of Tiftarea.

Muriel “Mimsy” G. Fournier 84, President of the Fournier & Fournier Funeral Home, died peacefully Saturday at home. She was born in Woonsocket a daughter of the late Arthur V. and Blandine (Beaudoin) Fournier. She was a tireless supporter of the Center and its programs and freely gave her time and energy as a treasurer, trustee, and advocate.Besides enjoying a great fondness for her family, she also enjoyed spending time with her friends, and playing cards at her favorite sanctuary at Spring Lake.She was the beloved aunt of Renee E. Darling and her husband Ron Miller with whom she lived, and was a special Nana to Renee’s children, Elliot and Kali Darling. She is also survived by a brother Robert Fournier Sr. and his wife Marie of Woonsocket, and a sister Lucille Rock-Wilcox and her husband George also of Woonsocket.She was also the cherished aunt of Denise Harker of Alphareta GA, Robert Darling Jr. and his wife Donna of Fort Pierce, FL, Robert Rock Jr. of Webster, NY, Marguerite Lemmerman and her husband Marc, of Chelmsford, MA, Michelle Fournier of Maui, HI, Robert Fournier Jr. and his wife Adrienne of Putnam CT, Richard Fournier and his wife Jen of Chicago IL, and Monique Keeley and her husband Kevin also of Chicago IL, and many great nieces and great nephews. She is predeceased by a brother Raymond Fournier, and a sister Lorraine Darling. She was a special woman of great faith with an uncommon grace that enriched the lives of all who knew her.She cherished her relationships with God, her family and her friends and will be deeply missed by many. Her funeral will be held Friday at 10:00 am from the Fournier & Fournier Home 99 Cumberland St., Woonsocket with a Mass of Christian Burial at 11:00 am in all Saints Church, Rathbun St., Woonsocket. Box 79, Woonsocket, RI 02895.

Richard D. Grady Sr., 75, of Manchester, died Saturday, Dec 15, 2007 after a period of declining health.	Born in Manchester on December 1, 1932, he was the son of John and Alexina (Hamel) Grady.  He attended Our Lady of Perpetual Help Grammar School and graduated from Bishop Bradley High School.  He served in the US Air Force and was stationed in Japan during the Korean Conflict.  He later served in the NH National Guard for many years.Prior to retirement, he had been employed at Green Thumb as a job developer.  Previous to that, he had been employed by the New Hampshire Union Leader as a photographer and had owned and operated the Queen City Auto School.  He found great joy in helping others and was an avid life long volunteer.  He had given over 4,000 hours of his time helping other veterans at the VA Medical Center in Manchester and also donated time and his photography expertise for New Horizons for New Hampshire.  Dick left many loyal and life long friends who will miss him dearly.  He will especially be missed by his Friday Photographers Lunch Bunch, Bob, Sam, Dick, Dick, Pat, and Aurel.  The family would like to thank all of his caretakers who assisted him during his time of declining health.Members of his family include three daughters, Beth Ann Grady, Ret. Air Force Major, of Augusta, ME, Mary Frances Helides of Raymond, and Amy F. Balban of Manchester; one son, Richard D. “T” Grady Jr. of Manchester; two grandchildren, Richard D. Grady III of New Boston and Molly M. Helides of Raymond; two step-grandchildren, Shaynon Baker and Tanya Baker Colon, both of Manchester; his twin brother, Robert P. Grady of Deerfield Beach, FL; a sister, Margaret A. Adams and her husband Bruce A. Adams of Bow; many nephews, nieces, cousins, and dear friends.He was predeceased by his parents, one son, Christopher E. Grady, and one brother, John C. Grady. A Mass of Christian burial was celebrated on Thursday December 20, 2007 in St. Catherine Church, corner of Webster St. and Hemlock St., Manchester.  Burial with full military honors followed in St. Joseph Cemetery, Bedford.In lieu of flowers, donations may be made to the VA Volunteer Department, 718 Smyth Rd, Manchester, NH 03104 or to New Horizons for NH, 199 Manchester St, Manchester, NH, 03101.
