DUDLEY- Patricia (Gardiner) Busch, 66, of 41 Lakeview Avenue, died Sunday, September 16, 2007, in Fairlawn Rehabilitation Hospital, Worcester. She leaves her husband of 44 years, Dr. Frederick L. Busch; a son, Lee F. Busch and his partner Jayme of Somerville; a daughter, Debra, wife of William Blakeman of Orange; her mother, Irma (Curn) Gardiner of Brewster, NY; a sister, Holly, wife of Barry Cohen of Brewster, NY; a grandson, Alex Blakeman; 4 nephews, Brice Wachterhauser, his wife Mary and their children Anya, wife of Jene Clark, and Kier Wachterhauser, Mark Wachterhauser, Scott Dravis and his family Debbie, Michael, Nicole, Kristine and Amanda, and Greg Dravis, his wife Cheryl and daughter Amy; 2 nieces, Terri Cohen and Dana, wife of Josh Molaver and their children Sydney and Jacob; special friends that she considered family, Marcia Wagner, Pamela Germaine and her husband and 2 children, Julie Margolis and her family, and many others. She was born in New York City and raised in Suffern, NY, the daughter of the late Jack Gardiner. She graduated from Suffern High School in 1958. She then earned a Bachelor of Arts Degree in Sociology from Temple University in Philadelphia in 1963. She also completed many graduate courses dealing with early education. She married her childhood sweetheart, her first and only love and soul mate, on September 7, 1963. She lived in Philadelphia until Fred graduated from the University of Pennsylvania with a VMD in 1965. They moved to New Jersey where he practiced veterinary medicine until 1968. They moved to Webster in 1968 and to Dudley in 1969. Mrs. Busch worked with her husband at his small animal hospital in Thompson from the time it opened until December 2003 when he retired. She was also a teacher at the Discovery House Preschool for over 20 years before retiring due to illness. There she displayed a special love and bonding for children, and again made lifetime friendships with her co-workers. She was an active and then honorary member of the Hubbard Regional Hospital Guild, where she had held many offices and had volunteered at the hospital gift shop. She was a member of the Tuesday Club, the Friends of the Pearle L. Crawford Memorial Library in Dudley and the Friends of the Chester C. Corbin Public Library in Webster. She was one of the 6 members of the Clara Barton Quilt Group, where she made lasting friendships with the quilters and their families.  Along with her husband, she was a longtime member of the International Wine and Food Society of Central Mass. and served as secretary of the organization for 15 years. She also belonged to a Worcester County wine educational group and held positions there. She enjoyed quilting, gardening and art, wine and food societies and traveling. Along with her husband, she supported many environmental organizations.A celebration of her life will be held Thursday, September 20, at 11:00 AM in Scanlon Funeral Service, 38 East Main St. Webster. Burial will be in Waldron Cemetery. Calling hours are from 5:00 to 8:00 Wednesday, September 19, in the funeral home. Donations in her name may be made to either Why Me Sherry’s House, 1152 Pleasant Street, Worcester 01602, or to the Dana Farber Cancer Institute Programs for Children, 10 Brookline Place, 6th Floor, Brookline, MA 02445.

WORCESTER____Reverend Albin J. Yankauskas, 84, of Worcester died Saturday September 15, 2007 in Radius Health Care, Worcester.He leaves several nieces, nephews and grand nieces and grand nephews. His brother, Paul Yankauskas, Jr., of California died earlier this year.  He was born in Worcester, a son of Paul and Amelija (Karosas) Yankauskas and graduated from Classical High School in 1942.  He studied for the priesthood at St. Thomas Seminary, Bloomfield, Ct., The Seminary of Philosophy and The Grand Seminary of St. Sulpice, both in Montreal, and was ordained a priest for the Diocese of Worcester on May 19, 1951 by Bishop John Wright in St. Paul’s Cathedral.  Father was an assistant pastor at parishes in Otter River, Athol, Millbury, Leominster, East Douglas and Worcester. He was appointed administrator of Our Lady of Vilna Church, Worcester from 1966 - 1968.  He was then named Pastor of St. Francis Church, Athol and served there until 1971. From 1973 to 1974 he was Pastor of St. Joseph Church, Berlin. He then was named Pastor of St. Aloysius Church in Gilbertville.  He retired from active ministry in 1986. Father was a fourth degree member of Bishop O’Reilly Assembly, Knights of Columbus, was a member of Council 116, Knights of Lithuania and had also served as chaplain for the Lithuanian Scouts.   The funeral will be held on Friday September 21, 2007 with a Mass at 10:00 a.m. in St. Casimir Church, 41 Providence Street.  Most Reverend George E. Rueger will be the principal celebrant.  Burial will be in Notre Dame Cemetery.  Calling hours for Fr. Yankauskas will be held in St. Casimir Church, 41 Providence Street, on Thursday September 20, 2007 from 5:00 to 7:00 p.m. The wake service will be held at 7:00 p.m., Monsignor Edmond T. Tinsley will preside. In lieu of flowers, memorial contributions may be made to the Clergy Benefit Plan, 49 Elm Street, Worcester, MA., 01609. DIRSA-MORIN FUNERAL HOME, 298 Grafton St., is directing funeral arrangements.

Marie Joan Cerasuolo, well known for her community outreach, died on Saturday September 15. She was 83. Marie was born to Giovanni and Concetta Bruno in East Boston. Her family moved to Medford in her teens. She married Frank Cerasuolo and moved to the Hillside area of Medford, where she lived the rest of her life. Marie found her passion in Christian worship, which in turn inspired her to community outreach. Marie spent 10 years visiting MCI Cedar Junction with the “Prison to Praise” program, bringing Catholic communion, joyful Christian music and a message of forgiveness to the inmates. She spent 5 years working with people who put together and served sandwiches for the needy once a month with the “Bread of life” out of Malden. Mrs. Cerasuolo was a founding member of the Maranatha prayer group, which has had more than a thousand members over the last thirty-five years. She also began and hosted an interdenominational prayer meeting on Monday morning in her house for the last thirty years, with 25 to 50 people coming to pray every week. While material goods were often distributed to the needy at those meetings, the point was always the prayer. She also served for many years as lector and extraordinary minister of communion at the now closed Sacred Heart Church I Medford Hillside. Marie was a big believer in family. She loved to feed everyone and always filled both family and guests with many courses of delicious food and great desserts.  While Mrs. Cerasuolo was a homemaker for many years, she also held many jobs, from being a seamstress before she was married, to running a luncheonette in the South Station in Boston to working as a salesperson in Teresa’s Fashions clothing store in Winchester. With all her other duties she found time to serve several families as a mother’s assistant. Beloved wife of Frank V. Cerasuolo. Devoted mother of Vincent Cerasuolo and his wife Cheryl of Medford, Joan Orvosh of Reading, Michelle Rizzi and her husband Jim of Salt Lake City, Frances Cioffi and her husband Paul of Burlington. Also survived by 12 loving grandchildren, 3 loving great grandchildren and numerous foster children. Sister of Rose Cerasuolo and her husband Michael of Reading.  Funeral the from Dello Russo Funeral Home, 306 Main St., MEDFORD Wednesday at 9 AM followed by a funeral mass celebrated in St. Raphael Church, 512 High St., Medford, at 10 AM.  Relatives and friends are respectfully invited to attend.  Visiting hours Monday and Tuesday 4-8.  Services will conclude with burial at Oak Grove Cemetery, Medford.   In lieu of flowers memorial contributions may be sent in Marie’s name to the Curt´s Pitch for ALS, 75 McNeil Way, Suite 310, Dedham, MA 02026 or www.curtspitch.org

Frank Leon Harmon, 91, passed away on Saturday, September 15, 2007 in Dixie, LA, where he lived for the past 1 ½ years.  He was born to the late James Lewis and Camilla Cooper Harmon in Lafayette County, MS.  He and his wife of 69 years, Mildred Wood Harmon, were former residents of Water Valley, MS for 30 years.  He was a veteran of the U.S. Army serving from January 1943 to May 1945.  He graduated -with a BS from the University of Mississippi in 1938, MA from the University of Mississippi 1940, and doctorate studies at Oklahoma State University.  He served as a High School Principal and Basketball Coach at Crawfordsville, AR 1940-1942, Retail Services at Biloxi, MS 1945-1947, High School Principal at Lake Village, AR 1948-1954, Professor of Mathematics at the University of Arkansas at Monticello 1954-1959, and Professor of Mathematics at the University of Louisiana Monroe 1959-1976.  He was co-author with Dr. Dan DuPree of 5 mathematics text books, beginning with “Fundamental Concepts of Mathematics.”  This book had a special emphasis for elementary educators to teach mathematical concepts in elementary schools.  He was a member of the Rotary International – Paul Harris Fellow, Broadmoor Baptist Church in Shreveport, LA and a former member of First Baptist Church of Water Valley, MS.  He was the Director of the National Science Foundation summer institute for Secondary Teachers – 1969, Director of NASA Research Contract with the University of Louisiana Monroe – 1963, and Recipient of the National Science Foundation Grant – 1963-1964.The visitation will be held on Wednesday, September 19, 2007 from 1:00 to 2:00 p.m. at Seven Oaks Funeral Home.  The funeral service will be held at 2:00 p.m. on Wednesday, September 19, 2007 in Henry Chapel of Seven Oaks Funeral Home with Dr. Randy Bain presiding.  Burial will take place in Pinecrest Memorial Gardens in Water Valley, MS.Mr. Harmon is survived by his wife, Mildred Wood Harmon of Dixie, LA; two daughters, Camilla Harmon Baxter of Collierville, TN and Eleanor Daniels Hilsenrath of Fairfield Bay, AR; a son, Dr. Frank Wood Harmon of Dixie, LA; ten grandchildren, Elizabeth Baxter, Lesley Baxter Bonds, Kenneth Baxter, Dr. Michael Daniels, Paul Daniels, Alex Daniels, Holly Harmon Bissell, Hayley Harmon Bumpas, Jon Harmon and Kendall Harmon; and seven great grandchildren.Along with his parents, Mr. Harmon was preceded in death by two sisters, Mary Bell Harmon Vines and Beaulah Harmon Reed and three brothers, Wayne Harmon, Earnest Harmon and Dwight Harmon.In lieu of flowers, memorials may be sent to the First Baptist Church in Water Valley, MS or Broadmoor Baptist in Shreveport, LA.

Mary R. BenDavid...worked on Apollo Space Program; 91TEWKSBURY - Mary R. (Drogo) BenDavid, age 91, a former technician for an aerospace manufacturer, died Saturday, September 15, 2007 at her Carnation Drive home, following an extended illness. Tewksbury.  Visiting hours Tuesday from 4 to 7 p.m.  in the funeral home.   Interment will be private.  Kindly omit flowers.  Memorials to charity of your choice will be appreciated.
